# Security Policy (Enterprise)

If you discover a security vulnerability, do NOT open a public issue. Report privately to security@thinkcube.dev or use GitHub's private security advisory flow.

Required information
- Description of the vulnerability and impact
- Steps to reproduce (minimal repro)
- Affected versions and components
- Proof of concept (if available)
- Suggested remediation (optional)

Encryption and PGP
- For sensitive attachments, encrypt with the project's PGP key (see PGP-FINGERPRINT below). The public key is available from the security team on request.

Response timeline
- Acknowledgement: within 72 hours
- Initial assessment: within 7 business days
- Remediation plan: communicated within 14 days where feasible

Disclosure policy
- Coordinated disclosure will be followed. Security team will publish CVE and public advisory once fixes are released or within an agreed timeframe.

Contact
- security@thinkcube.dev

PGP-FINGERPRINT: 0123 4567 89AB CDEF 0123 4567 89AB CDEF 0123 45 (replace with real key)

Supported versions
- Only the last two major releases are actively supported for security fixes unless otherwise specified in release notes.

Do not include secrets in reports. Follow up on any additional evidence requests from the security team promptly.
